task_completion.description: Apache OpenOffice Writer is launched to create the compliance manual.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Apache OpenOffice Writer application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The document includes a title section with the required company identifiers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains the company name 'Great Lakes Freight Lines, Inc.' along with a USDOT number and MC number at the beginning of the document.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is included in the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A structured Table of Contents block (often with a gray background indicating an auto-generated field in OpenOffice) is visible, listing document sections.

task_completion.description: The document contains multiple main sections (Heading 1) and subsections (Heading 2) covering regulatory topics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document displays clear hierarchical formatting with large headings (Heading 1) and subheadings (Heading 2) discussing topics like hours of service, driver qualification, or vehicle inspection.

task_completion.description: The document includes data tables for Fleet Inventory and Emergency Contacts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least one or more structured grid tables are visible in the document, specifically containing fleet inventory data (e.g., vehicle types, VINs) and emergency personnel contacts.

task_completion.description: Page numbers are inserted into the document's footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom margin (footer area) of the document pages displays page numbers.

task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenOffice Writer window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'GLFL_DOT_Compliance_Manual.odt'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenOffice Writer GUI to create and format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with OpenOffice menus, toolbars, or dialogs (e.g., inserting tables, formatting headings, generating TOC) rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The document content is properly formatted as a manual, not just raw JSON pasted into the editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is formatted with distinct headings, paragraphs, and tables, rather than displaying raw JSON syntax (like curly braces and quote marks).

integrity.description: The agent used Apache OpenOffice Writer as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface clearly belongs to Apache OpenOffice Writer, identifiable by its specific icons, layout, and window title.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document must contain a title section with 'Great Lakes Freight Lines, Inc.', USDOT 1847293, and MC 682041. It requires an auto-generated Table of Contents, at least 7 Heading 1 sections, at least 12 Heading 2 subsections, and at least 3 data tables (which must include Fleet Inventory and Emergency Contacts). The footer must contain page numbers. The document should cover regulatory terms including '49 CFR', 'hours of service', 'driver qualification', 'drug and alcohol', 'vehicle inspection', and 'hazardous material'.
